title: Cost per wear: why premium basics are the better investment
excerpt: The math that changes how you think about clothing budgets — and why one good hoodie beats five cheap ones.

sections.heading: The cost per wear calculation
sections.body: Cost per wear is simple: take the price of a piece and divide it by the number of times you realistically wear it. A $35 hoodie worn twice before it pills and loses shape costs $17.50 per wear. A $128 heavyweight hoodie worn 120 times over three years costs $1.07 per wear. The expensive piece is the cheaper one.

sections.heading: What degrades fast and what does not
sections.body: Thin fabrics, weak dyes, and lightweight construction degrade quickly under regular washing and wear. A heavyweight jersey with ring-spun cotton holds its shape and color through hundreds of wash cycles. Dense fleece with a brushed interior feels better after 50 washes than a thin sweatshirt does after five. Material weight is the single most predictive factor of longevity in casual wear.

sections.heading: The wardrobe math
sections.body: A wardrobe of 15 high-quality pieces costs more upfront than a wardrobe of 45 low-quality pieces. But the 15-piece wardrobe gets more daily use, looks better longer, and requires less replacement. After three years, the cost math often favors the premium wardrobe by a significant margin — and the storage, decision fatigue, and visual clutter savings are real additional benefits.

sections.heading: Jewelry holds differently
sections.body: Stainless steel with PVD finish is effectively permanent if maintained properly. A chain necklace or signet ring worn daily for five years costs almost nothing per wear — the cost is entirely front-loaded. This is why jewelry is one of the highest-value categories for cost-per-wear thinking. One good chain necklace at $98 worn daily for three years is less than ten cents per day.

sections.heading: What to prioritize first
sections.body: Start with the pieces you wear most frequently. Your daily tee, your daily outerwear, and your daily jewelry are the highest-ROI investments. These are the items where cost per wear compresses fastest. Statement pieces and seasonal items are secondary — invest there after the daily rotation is solid.

cta.heading: Heavyweight construction. Fair price.
cta.body: The Obsidian Drop uses premium materials engineered for real daily wear — not aspirational display.
